name: Norske Skog Union
bransje: Produksjon av papir og papp
ets_flag: ETS verified (70,000 t) differs from reported CO₂ (320,000 t) by 78.1%
narrative: Norske Skog Union is a pulp/paper facility in Skien in the Grenland cluster, emitting ~320 kt/y CO₂ (81% biogenic (reported)). High biogenic CO₂ share from biomass combustion; recovery boiler flue gas is concentrated and amenable to capture. Port access available.

name: Mongstad raffineri
bransje: Produksjon av raffinerte petroleumsprodukter og fossile brenselsprodukter
narrative: Equinor's Mongstad refinery is Norway's largest single industrial CO₂ source at ~1.85 Mt/y (100% fossil). The Marine Terminal provides world-class logistics; Equinor's existing CCS test centre (TCM) on site creates strong infrastructure synergies. Primarily a fossil CO₂ source, but scale and cluster infrastructure make this the leading large-scale site for CO₂ capture integration.

name: Gassco AS Kårstø prosessanlegg
bransje: Utvinning av naturgass
narrative: Kårstø is Equinor's largest onshore gas processing terminal (~1.15 Mt/y CO₂, 100% fossil). Existing LNG export infrastructure, dedicated power supply, and deep-water quay make this a Tier-1 logistics site. CO₂ is fossil, but the scale of operations and Equinor's CCS track record create a credible pathway for CO₂ utilisation.

name: Heidelberg Materials Brevik sementfabrikk
bransje: Produksjon av sement
narrative: Norcem/Heidelberg Materials Brevik is Norway's largest cement plant and highest-priority CO₂ source at ~720 kt/y (12% biogenic from alternative fuels). The Longship CCS project began capturing ~400 kt CO₂/y from 2025, reducing net available offgas; residual biogenic CO₂ and process integration opportunities remain for e-fuel co-location at Herøya.

name: Heidelberg Materials Miljø AS avd. Brevik
bransje: Energigjenvinning
narrative: Heidelberg Materials Miljø AS avd. Brevik is a waste-to-energy facility emitting ~720 kt/y CO₂ (12% biogenic (reported)). Located in Porsgrunn in the Herøya cluster, with port access available. Mixed-waste incinerator; biogenic fraction is significant and should be verified via monitoring plan.

name: Mo Fjernvarme
bransje: Vannbåren og luftbåren varme- og kjøleforsyning
narrative: Mo Fjernvarme operates district heating in Rana in the Mo Industripark cluster, emitting ~60 kt/y CO₂ (67% biogenic (reported)). Norwegian DH plants increasingly use biomass, elevating biogenic share. Port access available.

name: Mongstad kraftvarmeverk
bransje: Produksjon av raffinerte petroleumsprodukter og fossile brenselsprodukter
narrative: Mongstad kraftvarmeverk is a petroleum refinery in Alver in the Mongstad cluster, emitting ~800 kt/y CO₂. Fossil CO₂; however refinery sites typically have large power infrastructure and marine terminals. Port access available.

name: Yara Porsgrunn
bransje: Produksjon av gjødsel og nitrogenforbindelser
ets_flag: ETS verified (964,958 t) differs from reported CO₂ (755,016 t) by 27.8%
narrative: Yara Porsgrunn is Europe's largest ammonia plant, emitting ~755 kt/y fossil CO₂ plus 667 t/y N₂O (~198 kt CO₂-eq). CO₂ is 100% fossil (SMR), but co-location at Herøya with existing H₂ infrastructure, pipeline connections, and deep-water quay makes this a Tier-1 BD target for CO₂ offtake and green H₂ supply.

name: Hydro Aluminium Karmøy
bransje: Produksjon av aluminium

Enormous power infrastructure (3.5 TWh/y), dedicated port for ore/metal, and proximity to Equinor's Haugesund operations. CO₂ is fossil; however the scale of grid infrastructure is highly attractive for e-fuel co-siting.
